focus_areas: Vectorized multi-predicate filtering using AVX2: evaluate all 4 predicates (l_shipdate range, l_discount BETWEEN, l_quantity <) in a single branch-free loop; combined selectivity ~5% (60M→3M rows)

Morsel-driven parallelism: partition 60M lineitem rows into ~4K morsels; each thread: (1) vectorized filter evaluation on 10-15K rows, (2) accumulate partial sum per morsel in thread-local variable (no hash table needed, single global aggregate)
SIMD for discount BETWEEN check: use AVX2 packed compare (0.05≤discount≤0.07) + pack/compress to skip non-matching rows entirely
Final merge: each of 64 threads produces one partial sum, final reduction sums 64 values (negligible overhead, 1-2% of total time)
Column pruning: load only l_shipdate, l_discount, l_quantity, l_extendedprice (4 columns) from columnar storage
strategy_notes: Q6 is a high-throughput aggregation with complex filtering. The key insight is that filtering selectivity is low (~5%) but the input is large (60M rows). Use vectorized filtering to branch-miss minimally and process data in SIMD lanes. Since there's only 1 aggregation group (global sum), synchronization is trivial: each thread accumulates a thread-local sum without hash table overhead, then final merge uses atomic add or simple lock. Compile tight filter loop with SIMD intrinsics; unroll inner loop. Dictionary encoding on l_shipdate (delta) and other columns speeds up deserialization. Expected bottleneck: memory bandwidth for 60M row scan; SIMD filtering minimizes CPU overhead, letting I/O be bottleneck (good outcome).
reasoning: TPC-H SF10 is a parallel analytical workload dominated by large table scans (59M-60M rows) with low-cardinality aggregations. GenDB has 64 cores available but current performance is competitive only on Q1 (48ms vs 86ms DuckDB). Q3 (386ms) and Q6 (29.3ms) indicate suboptimal parallelism, join strategy, and filter vectorization. Primary optimization levers: (1) morsel-driven thread parallelism across all three queries, (2) SIMD vectorization for complex predicates (Q6 especially), (3) early filter pushdown in Q3 three-way join, (4) per-morsel partial aggregation to minimize synchronization overhead.
